inline_comments.message: The recommended way is to try importing the new package, then fall back to the old location on ImportError. See the discussion and last example in section https://docs.python.org/3/howto/pyporting.html#use-feature-detection-instead-of-version-detection

the python3 versions with list comprehensions work fine in python2.7, and can be done in single comprehensions, cf. https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/platform/plugins/+/98448/6/dmaap/dmaapcontrollerif/dmaap_requests.py#291
this is the dmaapplugin.dmaaputils module. I found it inconsistent to use a relative import here, and absolute in the line above.

dicts already support the 'in' operator for their keys themselves; there's no need to create a new list of the keys each time.

So, "feed_description" in ctx.node.properties is perfectly legal. In fact, even in python3 the keys() function returns an object that supports 'in' checks. https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict-views.

Using the keys() function is rarely ever needed. Dict itself supports len(), and list() over it does get the keys cf. https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/platform/plugins/+/98448/6/dnsdesig/dnsdesig/dns_plugin.py#37
Jack Lucas mentioned that version numbers should be incremented for all plugins.
only testenv:27 contains any commands, hence the py3x environments succeed without running any tests.
I do not see dev-requirements.txt being updated, and it contains the split coudify-4.1.1 packages in master.

It seems this is standard practice throughout ONAP, to fix a dependency in place for years.
The dependency on cloudify is a dependency of the package itself, not of the test environment. The package also depends on psycopg2(-binary as CI doesn't support installing it from source), which is not registered anywhere. Something the DCAEGEN2 plugins wanted to have in place on that PR. /rant
